reasoning: FIDE ratings update once a month on the 1st, so her rating on April 20 will mirror her rating on April 13, making a 1% (+25 point) increase practically impossible. For the later dates, a 25-point jump is substantial for a 2494-rated player and usually takes several months of consistent success or one breakout tournament. As an improving 21-year-old top junior, this is plausible over a 6-month horizon by October, but less likely in the immediate single update before the May rating list. Uncertainty increases with time as she will play more tournaments.
